titleStmt.title: The Chargé in the United Kingdom ( (Gallman) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chargé in the United Kingdom ( Gallman ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 97. 1. According Wright who asked Loy Henderson be informed soonest twenty year Anglo-Iraqi treaty was initialed this afternoon.The Department, on January 13, informed Cairo in telegram 40 (repeated to other Arab capitals and London) that it considered “agreement this general type in interests of US.” (741.90G/1–1348) Formal signature expected January 15.The treaty was formally signed at Portsmouth, England, on January 15; for text, see British Cmd. 7309, Iraq No. 1 (1948): Treaty of Alliance between His Majesty in respect of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and His Majesty the King of Iraq.
2. Embassy pouching text which Wright describes as “very satisfactory” and possible cornerstone on which future British relations with Egypt and other Middle East countries can be based.

3. Foreign Office tonight instructing British Embassy Jidda to outline substance treaty to Ibn SaudAbdul Aziz ibn Saud, King of Saudi Arabia. and to say that HMG would be pleased to make similar treaty with SAG. Visit FeisalAmir Faisal, Saudi Arabian Foreign Minister. London end January is suggested as possible opportunity for treaty talks.
